metadata.title: Why the Wave Function Has to Be Complex
metadata.note: Clean rebuild. Opposite phase gradients distinguish e^(+ikx) from e^(-ikx) despite identical density; probability current changes sign.

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.prompt: If their measured position probabilities are identical, where does left versus right live?
beats.label: If their measured position probabilities are identical, where does left versus right live?

beats.prompt: So the imaginary part is not decorative. Together with the real part, it lets the state encode a continuous phase angle and a nonzero probability flow.

beats.prompt: A localized packet works the same way. Two packets can share the same Gaussian envelope at one instant while opposite phase ramps give opposite mean momenta.

beats.prompt: Magnitude answers where. Complex phase answers how the probability flows. Lose the phase, and left and right collapse into the same incomplete picture.
